title: Temnadenia cordata
taxonomy: Temnadenia cordata is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae, commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. It is a member of the Apocynaceae family which comprises around 4,000 to 5,000 species. Within the Apocynaceae family, Temnadenia cordata belongs to the subfamily Plumerioideae. This subfamily contains around 45 genera, with Temnadenia being one of them. The classification of Temnadenia cordata reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants in the Apocynaceae family.

intro: Temnadenia cordata, also known as the 'Heartleaf' or 'Pituri', is a flowering plant in the Apocynaceae family native to Australia. The plant is known for its medicinal properties and has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including coughs, colds, and respiratory problems, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Temnadenia cordata include alkaloids, flavonoids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In herbal medicine, Temnadenia cordata is commonly prepared as a tea or tincture to alleviate respiratory issues, and is also used in traditional Indigenous Australian medicine for its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties.
